phase1.stop_reason: beam-search degeneration: empty predictions on all samples by epoch 4

sample_predictions.pattern: character spam on long sequences, correct on short
best epoch — short and medium nearly correct, long collapsed to empty
repetition collapse — model emitting cyclic ᚔᚌ pattern
complete generation collapse — empty predictions on all samples

phase1.failure_mode_summary: Beam-search degeneration consistent with exposure bias. Train and val loss continued to fall through epoch 4 (0.785 to 0.038 train, 0.406 to 0.442 val), but greedy/beam generation quality collapsed: epoch 2 was nearly correct on short and medium inputs, epoch 3 produced cyclic repetitions, and epoch 4 produced empty strings on all sampled inputs. The teacher-forced loss signal continued rewarding sharper LM-head distributions while autoregressive decoding found degenerate high-probability paths.
phase1.interpretation: TrOCR-base (385M params) does not productively transfer to this corpus under the tested optimisation regime. The 6x parameter scale-up over TrOCR-small reached an order of magnitude worse best CER (44.25% vs 0.06%) and could not be trained stably past epoch 2. Whether stricter regularisation (label smoothing, scheduled sampling, mixed teacher-forced training) could stabilise base-scale models is left to future work.

previous_attempts.outcome: diverged within first 3 epochs, CER climbed to 99-110%
loss decreased but generation barely started — CER 99.54% at epoch 1 with empty predictions; insufficient learning rate for productive convergence in available epochs

previous_attempts.config: LR 5e-6, batch 8, grad accum 4, 15 epochs (this run)
previous_attempts.outcome: best result at epoch 2: CER 44.25%, exact 25.8%; degenerated to empty predictions by epoch 4

comparison_to_trocr_small.headline: TrOCR-base reached 44.25% CER vs TrOCR-small's 0.06%, a gap of nearly three orders of magnitude. Scaling up parameters did not improve recognition on this Ogham corpus.
